Building a stable cryptocurrency for the real economy

Based on a proven blockchain protocol. Not reliant on external information or assets. Trustless and decentralised price stabilisation.

Eterna Protocol - Trustless and Decentralised Price Stabilisation

Eterna is a transactional cryptocurrency with a price stabilisation mechanism that relies on no external information or asset. The Eterna protocol is built to stabilise price without knowing what “price” is, and to do so without using any collateral, whether crypto or fiat. We believe that such fully “endogenous” mechanism is the only way to preserve the trustless and decentralised nature of the blockchain. Despite its internal complexity, Eterna is no more difficult to use than a regular cryptocurrency, and its stabilisation mechanism is inherently equitable, in that it automatically rewards people who support it.

Current Central Bank Deposit Rates (per annum)

Deposits earn interest in every block while they remain in the central bank. The rate of interest is recalculated in every block, separately for each of the deposit options. Since the central bank can issue new coins, it will never default on its obligations.

Eterna price stabilisation is built around the idea that the prospect of riskless return will entice Eterna holders to place coins that they are not actively using on deposit. Now suppose users expect Eterna price to fall – the interest they earn may no longer be sufficient compensation, and they will choose to withdraw their deposits with a view to selling Eterna. As soon as the central bank detects such outflows, it automatically raising interest rates, thus offering greater reward for keeping coins on deposit.

Deposit Balances

Thus, the central bank alleviates the pressure on Eterna price by decreasing the amount of coins available for sale. In other words, the Eterna protocol converts price volatility into interest rate volatility while, crucially, keeping money supply growth in check.

Desposit Inflows and Outflows

Based on a Proven Blockchain

We have chosen to base Eterna on a modified version of the Ethereum protocol (www.ethereum.org), inserting our price stabilisation mechanism on both the fundamental protocol level and through the smart contract architecture. All of Ethereum’s original smart contract capabilities have been preserved, as well as its PoW algorithm at the time of our launch and most of its other technical features.

We have made the decision to issue Eterna as a coin, rather than a token within Ethereum, since we saw this as the only way to successfully implement some of the crucial parts of our mechanism.

Mission and Strategic Priorities

Our mission is to advance the cause of self-sovereignty by contributing to creation of a truly effective and usable cryptocurrency. We aim to focus our efforts on attempting to solve the price stabilisation problem without resorting to any degree of centralisation or trust.

Our immediate strategic priorities are:

  1. Improve performance of the price stabilisation mechanism using data generated by the Eterna coin
  2. Improve computational efficiency of the protocol by removing monetary policy-related calculations from the EVM, thereby increasing effective block size
  3. [xxx]

Converting Price Volatility into Interest Rate Volatility

Eterna protocol involves a “central bank” that is native to the blockchain. In practice, it is a smart contract that has the ability to create new Eterna coins, and which offers several deposit options to all users – 12 hours, 3 days, 7 days, 14 days and 30 days. These durations signify the amount of time you will have to wait before you get your money back, from the time you ask for it.

How it works

1

Keep coins you need for daily transactions in your crypto wallet. Use Eterna for transactions as you would most major cryptocurrencies.

2

Place excess coins on deposit with the Central Bank.To pace coins on deposit, all you need to do is send them to a smart contract. Deposits are offered in five maturities: 12 hours, 3 days, 7 days, 14 days and 30 days. These periods of time signify how long you will have to wait before you get your deposit back after asking for it.

3

Earn riskless interest.Each of the deposits pays a different interest rate, determined in every block. The more popular a particular deposit is, the lower the rate that it pays. During periods when a large number of coins are withdrawn from deposits, interest rates will spike and you will earn even more.

4

Ask for your deposit back when you need it.To get you money back, all you need to do is send a message to the central bank smart contract. The smart contract will send it to your address with a delay that equals the maturity of your chosen deposit.
